## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey, it's Deepash and in this video, I want to give you a few examples of running the graduation framework and some of the things to look out for and how to go about Structuring it as well. So in this first example, I'm looking at an account where we ran the graduation framework for two different categories. So for this particular Account we had suits and jackets and therefore children and we tested both categories Against three different audience so look like a 1% of existing customers fans of friends and also fans as well directly To see how they would react to the creative and the products within that as well within each of the AdSets we were also Cycling different products is the lead product and to bring people to the category page for this specific Account and so you can see the permutations here. So jackets and suits and the different Audiences and then within the ad set itself we then ran multiple variants of those images as well So this is just a one month period just to demonstrate for this period We had three ads running but we have others lined up ready to test as well And there are different images different copy which we kind of test out through that as well And so if I go back into the ad set level just to illustrate kind of how the testing went on we were testing on 10 pound a day for these different ad sets now as best practice I try and aim for 5000 impressions to give me good confidence that these stats are sustainable and the CTR or CPC isn't just a one-off on the flip side I can tell pretty pretty early on if something is also not doing so well as well now The magic number for Facebook is 500 impressions. It's what Facebook uses for its calculation of relevance score and quite often There's two things to look out for number one if you don't get good performance in the first 500 impressions You could duplicate the ad set and try again because sometimes Facebook is throwing its hook into the ocean is put it in the wrong area and not brought back the right customers For me, this is a page post engagement objective And so I know that the the pool that Facebook is going into is going to be quite broad But based on my audience and but conversely if I see ad sets that are working quite well in particular These two ad sets for the lookalikes were working far better I wasn't really worried about testing these ad sets So that's something you can come back to if an ad set doesn't seem to be working You can always pause duplicate restart it to try and see if you can kick start it back in and what I'm looking at in particular Here is the cost per click and the click-through rate. So actually the Giz for the account are around 1.5 to 1.8 percent on click-through rate and the cost per click is kind of anywhere around the

## [03:03 - 06:08]

0.5 0.8 range. So When I'm running a page post engagement test I'm just looking for metrics that give me the evidence that on a page post engagement campaign The ad set and the creative are giving me good performance. So these two are good for me They've given me a good CPC and good CTR But because this has a funnel and you know, not all campaign objectives will but free commerce You can track further down the process. I want to actually see how many people are getting to cart How many people are actually buying so people buying on a page post engagement campaign is a strong indicator That you've hit the right audience and things are going well, but it's not the only indicator So for example here, we have this fans campaign which had a three pound twenty five cost per click 0.25 CTR actually did generate a 4.3 robust which obviously looks great, but the traffic is quite low and we you know I've discounted that out because that wasn't giving me the strong enough front-end metrics and that's what I'm looking at here if these strong if these metrics are good and I'm seeing actually good traction through and Really what I'm looking for is are people getting to car and how many as well? Then that that tells me that this audience and the creators within there within there are actually doing really really well as well So that for me gives me confidence that the audiences are ready now to go into a website conversion and the ads within that Whichever ones are performing are also ready as well so like if we just look at the ad level now and We look at the average of the ad set for CPC and CTR I want to identify which ones are actually performing better than the others So here you can see the CPCs are around the same mark So the ad set performance is generally doing well overall We have one ad here that has a far better click-through rate than the average and much better than the other two and if you look through the kind of metrics further through You look at the kind of add to cart rate is kind of generally on pile 6 over 108 through over 70 This one has got the higher Share of the budget and that's something to keep an eye on as well If you do want your other ad sets to get more traffic as you are spending more in your page post engagement testing To consider pausing the ads out given others a chance to cycle if you absolutely want to test ads To make sure that they're getting enough traffic then you can split the same ad set out three times For example run one ad in each I prefer to do it this way. It's quicker for me and it just means less Headache in managing multiple ad sets as well. So that's down to your particular preference So these three ads generally all performed well and they're pushing people through into the cart page as well So that for me is an ad set that's ready to be graduated plus all three ads are ready to be graduated The next step would be to pause these ads out and continue testing different ads And what you want to do at that point is once you've moved your ad set into a website conversion campaign

## [06:08 - 09:10]

It's then going to start becoming your money maker ads should be now pressing on and you've got three ads cycling through at some Point as you start to scale up those ads will start to fatigue So you want new ads to come in and that's where I'd be pushing more ads into the mix So that's to give an example of the graduation strategy for e-commerce You're looking at the how far people are getting down the metro that the funnel if you're getting this kind of CTR rate and people are bouncing off and you know you look at the Outbound clicks the landing page views if the people are not getting into a landing page view. That's not great If people are getting to landing page view and not into the car It's a signal that it may not be as successful in WC But like this if we are getting people through the funnel that's a strong indicator that will work in WC as well Now for lead gen where your funnel is probably smaller where you get someone to the landing page and sign up Then you know you want to look at people getting to the landing page You probably will get people to signing up as well But you know the gap between that might not be enough to look at funnel metrics for app install. I'm looking at running the ads in PPE and Sending them to the app install page and a recent change with Facebook is you can't put the app Link into your button on a page post engagement So I would put it into the body just to test the ads out and see if the ads are actually Attracting people into the app But with with app installs It's a lot faster to test because the app install objective is a lot broader than website conversion So the graduation strategy doesn't necessarily have to be used for app install I'd primarily use it for e-commerce and lead gen especially where there's a page to send lead gen off to as well So that's that example the second example I want to give is this account here where we've gone through and put Different interests in just to give you an idea of how this works at the interest level So the audiences are all the same so male female 21 to 55 Desktop and mobile and we're purely testing interests so within each of these ad sets They're running exactly the same ads and there's a range of video ads that are running in there And what we want to do is find the ads are performing best now in this account The average for click-through rates about zero point five percent and the average for cost per click is about 1 pound 50 to 2 pounds So we can see here that it isn't as good as the website conversion campaign But this is page post engagement and I want to find the indicators that are working So I can see here for example the the click-through rate for this particular interest and the one below so Gusto and holland and Barrett are Good comparatively to the rest and also against the account average and not far off CPC could be better But with this particular test we've given it enough impressions to give us confidence that we can sustain that and they would be too that

## [09:10 - 12:13]

I would graduate in terms of the Interest groupings into website conversion campaign to see if we can push it further and then to keep cycling those ads that Have been successful in page post engagement into the website conversion as well also when you are moving interests and you've tested interest by interest at the At this level when you move them into website conversion Where possible I would group them together at that stage now with these particular interests here If you know these companies or not some of these interests are quite different. So This particular interest is actually a fashion brand. This is vitamins and well-being fashion brand vitamins and a food delivery service. I would group those Personally into those groupings. So holland and Barrett the vital biotics The vital cocoa for example if they did graduate from PPE I would group those into the same interest in website conversion because if you know these interests are working Bigger traffic equals better response from website conversion. For example or Lee Jenner, whichever objective you're going for So I want to make sure that I'm giving as much as possible However, if your creative isn't different then group them all together The reason I would have done the or I do the interests separately is because for Fabuletics sweaty betty They're fashion brands and the creative that works for these people is different to the creative that works for the non-fashion brands So that's a reason to group those separately in my website conversion And then the kind of third example I want to give is a question on How much should I be spending of my budget on testing? When you're starting off your test budget will be a lot higher than when you're Skating for example, so this account for example, we were spending anywhere between 10 and 15 percent of our budget on testing and you can see right now of the 51 K that's gone in on our PPE test only 1400 has been spent which is a tiny percentage of the overall budget, but that budget is still being tested So it's still in the background that 1500 in this month is a lot higher than it was when we started out But that just gives you an idea that you can't have necessarily a set test budget Especially if you start to scale up you're spending 50 K a month 100 K a month 300 K a month It's not as easy to say, you know X percent is your test budget But really it's based on what you're testing within that and how many ad sets and how many things you're working on at the same time as well so for example if we have a look at this ad set this campaign here for example and let's have a look at the Click-through rates and we prioritise it by the biggest response on click-through rates So this account on average CPC we get about 0.8 dollars just under a dollar and click-through rate we get between kind of 1.5

## [12:13 - 15:14]

4.6 roughly so we can see here for example versus the overall for this test campaign This particular interest here has done really well both on CPC and click-through as well And we're getting clicks and we're getting some as to car as well So that's the kind of indicator that I'm looking at so again in this example the vitamin shop and Cosmetics would be ones that would be graduated ideal shape 310 nutrition as well and then you kind of go through and You know maybe nature box wouldn't be one that would go through So that's how I'd go through picking out which interests that I want to now graduate to my website conversion and In kind of this is another example of splitting out those tests and within that the ad sets I'm testing lots of different creatives as well So what you want to determine is is it my ad set that's not working or is it my creative and and so if I'm cycling creatives within the ad set and The ad set still doesn't perform It's probably the ad set that needs to be pivoted or changed or just paused out If you've tested for your ads and the ad sets starts performing Then you know that that particular ad is working and you know it could be the offer it could be the copy It's all things that you need to experiment and play with to try and get the response within that as well Now these ads that I've shown you are Carousel static summer video as well One question I've had is could you test using video views campaign? Yes, the video views objective is For all intents and purposes exactly the same as a page post engagement when it comes to reach and the type of people you're going for It's looking at video engagement obviously over a page post But the other advantage of video views is you get video Tools that you don't within the page post engagement. So if you're testing videos Specifically, there's no harm in doing that with a video views campaign using the same graduation strategy as we as I do in page post engagement and you can kind of then use what I would use is the cost per 10 second view and keeping that below 0.1 dollars or 10 cents essentially and seeing how many people actually getting what percentage are actually getting To that 10 second view or 25% as well Depending on how long your video is to give you the validation that the video and the ad set are working as well And could one final point is when it comes to testing this is a recommended structure You might have your own preferences for testing and bringing ads into your website conversion One of the questions is well, why can't I test in website conversion? And why use page post engagement or video views for testing? The point is that when you're running a website conversion campaign and you've got a given audience Let's say 1 million or 10 million whatever it is the website conversion Objective is just pulling out a small subset of that audience They're potentially ready to buy and if you haven't really tested your audiences and creators within that you could be discounting an audience out because

## [15:15 - 16:53]

They're in the buying mode, but your creative just didn't work for them when you go for a page post engagement It expands the audience out you've got more people to play with they may or may not be in market But they are still you know close to your ideal customer Facebook is it you know, it's a prediction engine It's predicting that someone is in buying mode or they're in browsing mode or they're in clicking mode or viewing mode or whatever it is And so for me PPE gives me a better range of those people. I might sometimes capture buyers I might sometimes just capture people are browsing But if they're interested then they will interact with my ad and that's really what I'm looking for and that's why this strategy works for me and it helps me to minimize the wasted spend in my App install campaigns in my lead gen campaigns in my website conversion campaigns to make sure I've tested things first So as a final recap Use page post engagement for testing and testing your graduation framework Test audiences test interests lookalikes, etc at your ad set level and give some kind of variation for your ads Give them Ideally, I'm aiming for five thousand impressions minimum to give me confidence that the numbers are sustainable Sometimes you'll see that within a thousand maybe two thousand impressions where the stats were all over the place the CPM the CPC, etc But that's the essential way that you want to be running the graduation strategy to make sure you're testing those ads and It's graduating the right ones to make your campaign as successful as it can be
